questions.q:
The correct order of decreasing acid strength of following acids is
(1) trichloro acetic acid
(2) trifluoro acetic acid
(3) acetic acid
(4) formic acid
questions.solution:
As \(-I\)-effect increases, \(-\mathrm{COOH}\) group becomes more electron deficient and tendency to loose \(\mathrm{H}^{+}\)ions increases i.e., acid strength increases. As \(+I\) effect increases. Acid strength decreases. Thus, correct order of acid strength is : trichloroacetic acid  >  trifluoroacetic acid  >  forminc acid  >  acetic acid.
Hence, the correct option is (c).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The chiral compounds among the following are
questions.solution:
The molecule is said to be chiral if the image of the molecule cannot be superposed on its mirror image by any combination of translations or rotations.

A chiral centre is atom to which four different groups are bonded in such a manner that it has a non super impossible mirror image.

 

Among the given structures except (IV) and (VI)  other don't have chiral centres.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
Consider the following three resonance structures
The correct order of their stabilities is
questions.solution:
A neutral structure with conjugation is more stable than the charged and unconjugated structures.

Thus, II is most stable.
Among I and III, I is more stable as it has conjugation of $\pi$-electrons and lone-pair electrons.

Thus, the stability order will be II $>$ I $>$ III.

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The order of stability of below resonance structures is
questions.solution: Non-charged molecular species are most stable. Therefore, (I) structure gives maximum stability. In II, III resonating structure, stability depends on distance between positive and negative charge. Minimum distance between both charge give maximum coluombic attraction. Thus, (II) resonating structure is more stable than (III). Hence, order of stability will be I $>$ II $>$ III.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion (A) $-\mathrm{NH}_2$ group of aniline is ortho, para directing in electrophilic substitutions.

Reason (R) $-\mathrm{NH}_2$ group stabilises the arenium ion formed by the ortho, para attack of the electrophile.
The correct answer is
options: Both $(A)$ and $(R)$ are correct, $(R)$ is the correct explanation of $(A)$
Both $(\mathrm{A})$ and $(\mathrm{R})$ are correct, $(\mathrm{R})$ is not the correct explanation of $(\mathrm{A})$
(A) is correct, but (R) is not correct
(A) is not correct, but (R) is correct
questions.solution: The $-\mathrm{NH}_2$ group of aniline is a very strong electron donor (+ $M$ effect), hence it activates the benzene ring thoroughly and the electrophilic aromatic substitutions on the benzene ring are very easy to take place at ortho and para-positions.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
The correct order of acidity of the following compounds is
(I) CH3-C≡CH
(II) F3C-C≡CH
(III) CH3C≡CCH3
(IV) CH3CH=CHCH3
questions.solution:
The acidity of unsaturated compounds can be best described by observing the substituted group. The compounds containing the substituted group having strong -I effect or week +I will be more acidic than the compounds containing the substituted group having week -I effect or strong +I effect.

This is in accordance with reactivity series of inductive effect.

So, the order of acidity in the following group of compounds will be:

II>I>III>IV
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Sodium fusion extract of aniline when heated with ferrous sulphate solution and then acidified with concentrated $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4$ from which of the following complexes?
questions.solution:
The carbon and nitrogen present in the organic compound of fusion with sodium metal give sodium cyanide $(\mathrm{NaCN})$ soluble in water. This is converted into sodium ferrocyanide by the addition of sufficient quantity of ferrous sulphate. Ferric ions generated during the process react with ferrocyanide to form the prussian blue precipitate of ferric ferrocyanide. The prussian blue colour is due to formation of ferric ferrocyanide, $\mathrm{Fe}_4\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]_4 \cdot X_2 \mathrm{O}$.
Reaction
(i)$\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}+6 \stackrel{\ominus}{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{N} \longrightarrow\left[\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}(\stackrel{-6}{\mathrm{CN}})_6\right]^{4-}$
Hexa cyanoferrate (II)
(ii) $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+} \underset{\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4}{\stackrel{\text { Conc. }}{\longrightarrow}} \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}+e^{-}$
(iii) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}(\stackrel{6}{\mathrm{CN}})_6\right]^{4-}+4 \mathrm{Fe}^{3+} \longrightarrow\left[\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}(\stackrel{6}{\mathrm{CN}})_6\right]^{4-} \cdot \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

$A$ and $B$ are enantiomers, $C$ and $D$ are diastereomers
$A$ and $B$ are diastereomers, $C$ and $D$ are diastereomers

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following statements are correct?
(i) Inductive effect and resonance effect is possible in chlorobenzene.
(ii) Resonance effect dominates over inductive effect in anisole.
(iii) $p$-nitrobenzoic acid is less acidic than $m$-nitrobenzoic acid.
(iv) Diphenylamine is more basic than aniline.
questions.solution:
(i) The $\mathrm{Cl}$ group activates benzene ring more by resonance effect. $\mathrm{Cl}$ group being more electronegative withdraws electrons from benzene ring and shows - I effect. (ii) The methyl group of ether can donate electrons through inductive effect that is not as strong as electron donation effect to the pi-system. So, resonance effect dominates over inductive effect in anisole.
(iii) $p$-nitrobenzoic acid is more acidic than $m$-nitrobenzoic acid. When both acids donate $\mathrm{H}^{+}$ from $-\mathrm{COOH}$ group, then $p$-nitrobenzoate is more stabilised due to $m$-effect of nitro group at meta position by deactivating the ring. So, statement (iii) is incorrect. (iv) Diphenylamine is less basic than aniline. The lone pair of $\mathrm{N}$-atom in diphenylamine is delocalised on two benzene rings due to which it is less available to donate resulting into decrease in basicity. In aniline, it is delocalised on one benzene ring, so lone pair is more available for donation. Hence, it is more basic. So, statement IV is incorrect.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For a compound with empirical formula \(\mathrm{C}_7 \mathrm{H}_8 \mathrm{O}\), how many aromatic structures are possible?
questions.solution:
For the compound having formula \(\mathrm{C}_7 \mathrm{H}_8 \mathrm{O}\) there are 5 aromatic isomers. (1) Benzyl alcohol (2) o-methyl phenol (3) \(m\)-methyl phenol (4) \(p\)-methyl phenol (5) Anisole
Hence, the correct option is (b).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Steam distillation process cannot be used for purifying which of the following?
questions.solution:
Steam distillation is the simplest method, which is used to isolate materials that are temperature sensitive like organic compounds. It is applicable at a low boiling point in order to avoid decomposition.
It cannot be applicable in the purification of those organic compounds that are water soluble, non-volatile in steam, vapour pressure low and contains volatile impurities. $p$-nitrophenol is steam volatile, thus it cannot be used for purifying by steam distillation.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In Lassaigne's test for halogens, it is necessary to remove <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>'</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>'</mo></math> from the sodium fusion extract, if nitrogen and Sulphur are present. This is done by boiling the extract with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>'</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi><mo>'</mo></math>. Identify <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi></math>.
questions.solution:
Lassaigne's extract is used for the detection of nitrogen and sulphur. In this the organic compound reacts with the sodium thiocyanate followed by the Fe3+ cation and formed the [Fe(SCN)]2+ complex which is red blood colour.

For the test of halogens, it is necessary to remove the sodium cyanide and sodium sulphide. So, the compound X is sodium cyanide and compound Y is sodium sulphide. These can be removed by the nitric acid.

NaCNX + HNO3Z→NaNO3 + HCNNa2SY + 2HNO3Z→2NaNO3 +2H2S

Hence, X=NaCN;Y=Na2S ;Z= conc. HNO3.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
